tldr: The proposal studies a scoped bridge plus an exact quartic law program. After fixing one homogeneous linear-in-share block-fixed isolated-cluster specialization of the Cai randomized-saturation expansion and naming its resulting coefficient tuple, lem:variance-moment-reduction identifies the o(N^-1)-free leading comparison of every feasible saturation vector with the quartic functional V evaluated at its empirical law, and proves that along regimes M,m -> infinity with N pbar in Z the sharp feasible asymptotic leading surrogate equals min_{nu in N(pbar)} V(nu) via support-at-most-three reduction plus grid-split rounding. The unconditional theorem-level contribution is the quartic-program theory itself: a global dual/KKT certificate, a closed-form threshold for the stratified law delta_pbar, a complete closed-form optimizer over the singleton/two-point class, a support-at-most-three optimizer reduction, finite-M attainability with O(M^(-1)+m^(-1)) rounding implementation, and a special-purpose terminating exact optimizer/certificate procedure that uses elimination/CAD/Thom machinery at exact symbolic or algebraic inputs. No separate parameter-uniform branch-atlas theorem is claimed. The remaining directions are the compact human-readable symbolic atlas and the exact non-leading finite-population variance.

project_justification.gap: Randomized-saturation papers derive leading variance expressions and optimize inside finite or structured design families, but they do not fix one homogeneous isolated-share specialization of that expansion and then solve the induced bounded-support quartic law program globally over the full admissible law class.
project_justification.niche: The live niche is the exact design geometry of the isolated homogeneous within-cluster leading criterion: a profiled nonconvex moment problem sitting strictly between generic moment-set theory and applied randomized-saturation heuristics.
project_justification.fill: Conditionally on a fixed bridge transcription from the Cai expansion, the project proves that along regimes M,m -> infinity with N pbar in Z the feasible asymptotic leading criterion equals the quartic law optimum min_{nu in N(pbar)}V(nu), with the optimizer reached by support-at-most-three reduction plus O(M^(-1)+m^(-1)) rounding; independently of that bridge, it proves the global dual/KKT certificate for the quartic program, gives the closed-form threshold for the stratified delta_pbar design, proves the complete singleton/two-point optimizer formula, and gives a special-purpose terminating exact algebraic optimizer/certificate procedure for exact symbolic or algebraic inputs.

related_work: Hudgens and Halloran (2008) introduced the two-stage partial-interference design framework and the causal contrasts that make randomized saturation meaningful. Baird, Bohren, McIntosh, and Ozler (2017) place randomized saturation in a regression and power-calculation framework and derive design tradeoffs over finite saturation menus, but they do not solve the full bounded-support law problem. Basse and Feller (2016) study finite two-stage analysis with unequal cluster sizes, weighting, and variance estimation; the present proposal instead contributes an optimizer-preserving attainability and rounding theory. Cai, Pouget-Abadie, and Airoldi (2022) are the closest comparator: they derive closed-form bias and variance under a linear interference model, optimize randomized designs analytically within structured families, and supply the expansion template used here. The present note does not claim to derive from first principles a universal law-level criterion from that paper; rather, it fixes one homogeneous isolated-share block-fixed specialization of the Cai expansion and then makes the induced unrestricted bounded-support moment program itself the theorem object. On the unconditional algorithmic side, the contribution is deliberately narrower: an exact CAD/Thom solver-certificate specialized to this quartic program, not a broadly adopted field-level design rule. Offer-Westort and Dimmery (2021) show that interior treatment-correlation designs can beat endpoint rules for homogeneous-policy estimands, reinforcing that the endpoint benchmark is not structurally safe. Pinelis (2012) and Winkler (1988) supply the finite-atomic geometry for linear moment slices, but they do not handle the profiled outer nonlinearity created by -V_4 m_2(nu)^2. Lu, Shi, and Ding (2026) mark the current frontier in broader spillover settings and clarify that the present proposal is a deliberately specialized exact-design theorem under isolated within-cluster interference rather than a solution to general network experimentation.
honest_scope: The modeling assumptions enter only through a scoped leading design-variance bridge and the design-optimality corollary. The bridge fixes one chosen homogeneous isolated-share specialization of the Cai expansion and proves that, along regimes M,m -> infinity with N pbar in Z, each feasible design has leading value V at its empirical law and the sharp feasible asymptotic optimum equals min_{nu in N(pbar)}V(nu) via support-at-most-three reduction plus rounding; it is not independent theorem-level progress beyond that transcription, nor a broader randomized-saturation design theorem. The global certificate, stratified-threshold theorem, singleton/two-point optimizer, and SUTVA corner are pure statements about the quartic V program. The closed-form threshold is for the stratified law delta_pbar specifically, with the skewed witness showing why that branch can fail. The branch solver delivers a complete exact algebraic optimizer and certificate only for exact symbolic or algebraic inputs, using elimination/CAD internally; no standalone parameter-uniform branch atlas over Theta is claimed. Prop:explicit-two-point-cell fully solves the singleton/two-point class. The exhaustive human-readable region-by-region symbolic atlas and the exact non-leading finite-population variance are the residual directions.
